gaps: Part 3 pricing logic field: spec requires each tier to have explicit 'pricing logic — what the price is anchored against'; Lite's anchor ('your current price — kept as the entry anchor') is thin and doesn't articulate what it's anchored against beyond status quo

Vague-input preamble used despite user providing a concrete $400 anchor and 'resume writing' arguably qualifying as a named sub-niche with known norms; the assumption stub about vertical is fine but the framing implies tokens were considered when $ figures were appropriate

Ceiling logic ($2,200) sits below the Premium flagship ($2,400), which is coherent for routing but should explicitly state it routes *to Premium* as the spec phrases it ('above which auto-route to Premium or custom') — wording is present but the numeric relationship to flagship could be clarified
suggested_fixes: Add explicit anchor sentence for Lite (e.g., 'Anchored at current market entry for single-pass professional rewrites; preserves existing client expectations')
Drop or tighten the preamble since $400 is concrete — replace with a one-line scope assumption only if needed
Clarify ceiling: 'Ceiling: BYO >$2,200 routes to Premium (since Premium flagship at $2,400 delivers more for marginal cost)'

gaps: Follow-up menu offers three options (a/b/c) instead of the spec's 'offer one based on context'
Pricing-anchor table proliferates tokens (+$A through +$L) beyond the spec's example of +$A/+$B/+$C, which risks looking fabricated-by-volume rather than cleanly placeholder
suggested_fixes: In 'Optional Follow-Up Menu' execution, pick the single highest-value follow-up (here: re-anchor with real prices) rather than presenting a multiple-choice menu
Consolidate BYO upgrade tokens into a smaller set (e.g., +$D/+$E/+$F) or label them as 'illustrative deltas' more explicitly to avoid token sprawl

gaps: Part 3 Flagship-protection check: Growth row math is incoherent — uses a '+$500 (half-brief proxy)' that contradicts the very rule (no half-briefs in BYO) introduced in the same section, and the totals ('$5,000+' then 'adjust to $5,500') are hand-waved rather than cleanly derived

Part 3 Flagship-protection: Growth tier protection margin is marginal/tied even after adjustment ($500), violating the spirit of 'flagships must remain the rational discount' — the per-dimension prices should have been calibrated upward so Growth's BYO-equivalent clearly exceeds $5,000 without retroactive proxies

Part 3 BYO placement: spec requires BYO slotted 'underneath the cheapest and between existing tiers,' but the floor is set equal to Starter ($2,000) rather than underneath it, collapsing the 'underneath' slot into a routing redirect instead of a real sub-Starter BYO option

Part 3 Configuration column missing for existing tiers in dimension-language: tiers are restated in original prose but not mapped onto the Part 2 dimensions (Tracking/Cadence/Access/Content/Links/Tech), making the flagship-protection sum-check harder to audit

Memory Integration: no memory preamble or note about availability — should either include preamble or silently omit; current output is ambiguous (acceptable per spec, but worth confirming silent omission was intentional)
suggested_fixes: Recompute Growth BYO-equivalent cleanly: price the included brief as a standalone $1,200 BYO line (no half-brief proxy), yielding $2,000+$800+$1,200+$500+$1,200 = $5,700 vs $5,000 flagship — Growth wins by $700
Raise Cadence: Biweekly+call to +$1,500 or Access: Slack to +$700 so Growth's BYO-equivalent clears flagship by ≥15%, matching the Pro tier's protection margin
Set BYO floor below Starter (e.g., $1,200/mo for 5kw + quarterly report only) to genuinely slot 'underneath the cheapest,' then route upward to Starter at $2,000

Add a 'Configuration (in dimension terms)' sub-column to the existing-tiers table so each tier's Tracking/Cadence/Access/Content/Links/Tech levels are explicit and the BYO sum check is line-by-line auditable
Explicitly state 'Memory unavailable — preamble omitted' or remove ambiguity by confirming silent omission per spec

gaps: Memory Integration: spec says 'If memory is unavailable, omit the preamble silently' — output includes 'Memory unavailable — proceeding without preamble.'
BYO Per-dimension upgrades: Breadth upgrade line ('Light → Standard +$9K → Heavy +$11K (additive)') is ambiguous — unclear if Heavy total is +$11K or +$20K
Flagship-protection math: Core BYO sum stated as ~$50K is not transparently itemized from the listed upgrades, making the 15-20% discount claim unverifiable inline
suggested_fixes: Remove the 'Memory unavailable' line entirely when memory isn't available, per the silent-omit rule
Restate Breadth upgrade unambiguously, e.g., 'Standard +$9K, Heavy +$20K (cumulative from base)'
Show a one-line BYO sum breakdown for Core and Atelier configs to make flagship-protection math auditable

gaps: Part 3 tier table/pricing-logic format: spec example shows tier rows with explicit price + anchor; output uses prose blocks with token prices, which is acceptable but Tier 1 ($0) lacks a 'discount vs. flagship' framing and Tier 2/3 anchors mix justification with pricing logic rather than cleanly separating
Vague-input preamble names two unlock variables (buyer + revenue benchmarks) but spec format implies one specific variable; minor drift from the single-variable stub pattern
Flagship-protection rule is stated as a percentage band rule rather than demonstrated — no worked sum showing BYO-to-Practitioner > $B, so the rule is asserted not proven
Follow-up menu offers three options ('a/b/c') but spec says offer ONE based on context
suggested_fixes: Render Part 3 tiers as a table with columns Tier | Price | Anchor matching the inline example's format for consistency
Tighten preamble to a single unlock variable (e.g., 'once you name target buyer') rather than listing two
Add a one-line worked check: 'BYO sum to Practitioner config = base + $b1 + $s_async ≈ 1.2×$B ✓' to demonstrate flagship protection
Pick one follow-up (most likely pricing validation or configurator export) instead of presenting a three-option menu
